title: A Survey on Vision-Language-Action Models for Embodied AI
authors: Yueen Ma; Zixing Song; Yuzheng Zhuang; Jianye Hao; Irwin King
year_basis: First public appearance: arXiv:2405.14093 in 2024; later published in IEEE TNNLS with a 2025 DOI
venue: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
long_horizon: N/A — survey reviews long-horizon task planners
language_conditioned: N/A — language conditioning is central to the surveyed field
human_robot_interaction: N/A — survey covers user instruction following and embodied interaction
uses_world_model: N/A — survey reviews world models as VLA components
world_model_detail: Reviews latent dynamics, LLM-induced world models, visual world models, and related dynamics-learning components.
uses_planning: N/A — survey reviews low-level policies and high-level task planners
horizon_basis: The survey treats hierarchical task decomposition as the dominant current approach to long-horizon VLA tasks.
observation_modality: Multiple: vision; language; state; action; 3D; multimodal sensing
goal_representation: Primarily language; multiple goal forms reviewed
benchmark: Multiple real-world and simulation datasets, simulators, and VLA/task-planning benchmarks
main_contribution: Provides a VLA taxonomy spanning components, low-level control policies, and high-level task planners, with associated datasets, simulators, and benchmarks.
novelty: Explicitly organizes VLA research around a hierarchical split between action-generating control policies and long-horizon task planners.
limitations: The surveyed field faces unresolved safety, benchmark coverage, generalization, multimodal alignment, long-horizon integration, latency, and multiagent coordination challenges.
future_work: Develop unified end-to-end long-horizon VLA systems, broader benchmarks, safer and faster deployment, stronger foundation-model generalization, and richer multimodal sensing.
evidence_world_model: The survey defines world models as predictors of future states conditioned on states and actions and reviews latent, textual, and visual variants.
evidence_planning: It separates high-level task planners from low-level VLA control policies and reviews monolithic and modular planners.
evidence_horizon: The survey states that hierarchical systems are currently the most practical approach for long-horizon tasks but introduce latency and additional failure points.
review_flags: Exclude from method-only model and performance counts; retain for survey trends, taxonomy comparison, and long-horizon gap analysis.
source_document: A Survey on Vision--Language--Action Models for Embodied AI.txt

title: Causal-JEPA: Learning World Models through Object-Level Latent Masking
authors: Heejeong Nam; Quentin Le Lidec; Lucas Maes; Yann LeCun; Randall Balestriero
year_basis: First public appearance: arXiv:2602.11389 in 2026; ICML 2026 publication
open_source_notes: Code repository reported by the paper: https://github.com/galilai-group/cjepa
world_model_detail: Object-centric JEPA with a frozen visual/object encoder and a masked transformer predictor trained on history completion and future latent prediction.
prediction_target: Masked historical object states and future object-centric latent states
horizon_basis: MPC uses H=5 action blocks of size B=5, producing a 25-action optimized sequence; the evaluation episode budget is 50 steps.
policy_type: None — actions are selected by MPC rather than a learned policy
pretraining: Yes — frozen DINOv2-derived visual features and object-centric encoder
goal_representation: Goal observation encoded as an object-centric latent state
main_contribution: Introduces object-level latent masking in a JEPA world model to make interaction-dependent prediction necessary while greatly reducing planning token cost.
novelty: Treats masked object states as counterfactual-like observability interventions, combining object-centric abstraction with reconstruction-free joint latent prediction.
limitations: Performance is bounded by the object-centric encoder, influence neighborhoods are not validated against explicit temporal causal graphs, and evaluation is limited to relatively controlled environments.
future_work: Jointly refine stronger object-centric encoders without collapse and evaluate in more complex environments with richer interactions and explicit causal structure.
evidence_world_model: The predictor completes masked object histories and predicts future object latents, optionally conditioned on actions and proprioception.
evidence_planning: Push-T actions are optimized with CEM and repeatedly executed in an MPC scheme using predicted latent goal error.
evidence_horizon: The optimized sequence contains 25 low-level actions, which is medium under the dataset rule and is not task-level long-horizon planning.
review_flags: Core JEPA/world-model paper. Do not equate its 25-action MPC horizon with long-horizon task decomposition.
source_document: Causal-JEPA_ Learning World Models through Object-Level Latent Interventions.txt

title: Continuous control actions learning and adaptation for robotic manipulation through reinforcement learning
authors: Asad Ali Shahid; Dario Piga; Francesco Braghin; Loris Roveda
open_source_notes: Software repository reported by the paper: https://github.com/Asad-Shahid/Intelligent-Task-Learning
world_model_detail: N/A — PPO and SAC are used as model-free reinforcement-learning algorithms.
horizon_basis: A closed-loop policy directly executes a single grasp-and-lift task; no explicit planner or task decomposition is used.
pretraining: Yes — a base grasping policy initializes adaptation to modified objectives
observation_modality: State: robot proprioception; object pose; relative object-gripper position
action_space: Continuous: seven joint-velocity commands plus gripper command
benchmark: Custom MuJoCo grasping and obstacle-avoidance tasks
main_contribution: Compares PPO and SAC for continuous robotic grasp control and proposes sequential fine-tuning to adapt a learned policy to additional performance and safety objectives.
novelty: Demonstrates model-free policy adaptation with an unconstrained continuous joint-command action space and zero-shot transfer from simulation to a real Franka robot.
limitations: The approach still depends on simulated training, uses engineered state information rather than vision, and evaluates a narrow grasping task; direct real-world learning remains sample inefficient.
future_work: Study rigorous one-shot versus fine-tuning comparisons, model-based and meta-RL adaptation, dynamic targets and obstacles, human-aware safety, multiagent modular control, and visual environment updates.
evidence_world_model: The paper explicitly uses model-free PPO and SAC and does not learn predictive environment dynamics.
evidence_planning: The stochastic policy maps the current 46-dimensional state directly to continuous robot commands without search or trajectory optimization.
evidence_horizon: The evaluation is a single reach-grasp-lift behavior with additional control constraints, not a long-horizon task plan.
review_flags: Supporting model-free comparison; peripheral to core world-model and explicit-planning analyses.
source_document: Continuous control actions learning and adaptation for robotic manipulation through reinforcement learning.txt

title: DayDreamer: World Models for Physical Robot Learning
authors: Philipp Wu; Alejandro Escontrela; Danijar Hafner; Ken Goldberg; Pieter Abbeel
year_basis: First public appearance: arXiv:2206.14176 on 28 Jun 2022; CoRL 2022
open_source_notes: The authors publicly release the experiment infrastructure and provide a project website.
primary_problem: World Modeling; Model-Based Reinforcement Learning; Policy Learning
world_model_detail: Dreamer recurrent state-space model with multimodal encoder, stochastic latent dynamics, observation decoder, reward model, and actor-critic behavior learning.
planning_method: Policy Rollout — actor-critic optimization from imagined latent trajectories
horizon_basis: Imagined actor-critic rollouts use H=16 steps; the critic bootstraps beyond that horizon to learn longer-term strategies.
learning_type: Model-Based Reinforcement Learning; Online Real-World Learning
pretraining: No — each task is learned online from scratch
observation_modality: Multimodal: RGB cameras; proprioception; velocity and task-specific sensors
goal_representation: Reward-defined task objective; explicit goal position for navigation
action_space: Multiple: continuous and discrete across platforms
benchmark: Custom real-world A1 walking; UR5 and XArm pick-and-place; Sphero navigation
main_contribution: Demonstrates that Dreamer can learn locomotion, manipulation, and navigation directly on four physical robots without simulators or demonstrations.
novelty: Establishes a common real-world world-model baseline across diverse robots and sensing/action configurations using the same hyperparameters.
limitations: Multi-hour hardware learning causes robot wear and possible human intervention, experiments may not expose the method's ultimate limits, and the evaluated tasks remain comparatively constrained.
future_work: Train longer, tackle more challenging tasks, and combine fast real-world world-model learning with the benefits of simulation.
evidence_world_model: An action-conditioned RSSM learns latent dynamics from replayed real-world experience and predicts trajectories used for behavior learning.
evidence_planning: The actor and critic are optimized on imagined latent rollouts rather than by deployment-time MPC or search.
evidence_horizon: The imagination horizon is 16 steps; this supports long-term control strategies but not high-level long-horizon task decomposition.
review_flags: Core world-model baseline. Count as imagination-based policy planning, not deployment-time trajectory optimization.
source_document: DayDreamer_ World Models for Physical Robot Learning.txt

title: Dexterous Manipulation for Multi-Fingered Robotic Hands With Reinforcement Learning: A Review
open_source_notes: The article is open access, but no companion code or curated dataset repository is identified.
uses_world_model: N/A — not focused on learned world models
world_model_detail: Reviews classical analytical modeling as well as model-free RL, learning from demonstration, and sim-to-real transfer.
uses_planning: N/A — reviews classical grasp and motion planning
horizon_basis: The review does not present one implemented planner or a normalized planning horizon.
observation_modality: Multiple: proprioception; vision; tactile and other sensors discussed
benchmark: Multiple dexterous-hand tasks and simulation environments
robot_platform: Multiple: Shadow Hand; Allegro; TriFinger; DClaw; Utah/MIT; DLR/HIT II and others
main_contribution: Reviews classical modeling/planning/control and reinforcement-learning approaches for multi-finger dexterous manipulation, including demonstrations and sim-to-real transfer.
novelty: Concentrates specifically on reinforcement-learning methods for multi-fingered hands and connects them to earlier analytical manipulation methods.
limitations: The field remains constrained by sample inefficiency, exploration difficulty, sim-to-real gaps, high hardware cost, poor generalization, limited sensing, and inability to perform many complex tasks.
future_work: Improve simulators, sensor fusion, algorithms and adaptation, semantic instruction understanding, robotic-hand hardware, manipulation in non-air media, and foundational contact theory.
evidence_world_model: The review discusses analytical models and reinforcement learning but is not organized around learned predictive world models.
evidence_planning: It reviews high-level grasp and motion planning as background rather than proposing a single planning architecture.
evidence_horizon: No paper-level long-horizon planning method or task decomposition is evaluated.
review_flags: Peripheral to the core corpus; useful as historical dexterous-manipulation context but should be excluded from core world-model and long-horizon method counts.
source_document: Dexterous Manipulation for Multi-Fingered Robotic Hands With Reinforcement Learning_ A Review.txt
